title: papaver alpinum subvar. aurantiacum
intro: Papaver alpinum subvar. aurantiacum, also known as the Alpine Poppy, is a subspecies of the Papaver alpinum plant, a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid compounds, particularly papaverine and thebaine. The health benefits of Papaver alpinum subvar. aurantiacum include its ability to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it should be noted that the plant's use is largely limited to folk medicine and its efficacy has not been extensively scientifically proven.
